item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Shame the image got nuked. Also one would think the foundation would contain them in a city environment since they're friendliest towards researchers

+1 tho

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The concept is really interesting, and the article has the perfect size for digesting the concept. This really fits into the odd collection of Wondertainment items. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Interesting concept, but the article itself is bad. Awful use of redactions and those LOLFoundation notes are bad…

-1

item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded: Image originates from here, which is under All Rights Reserved. As such, it is not compliant with CC 3.0, and has since been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I know its a bit old to post here, but I really like this SCP. Especially the experiment logs, those are really interesting to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Would be interesting to see what happened if something swallowed the egg.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Always been a personal favorite of mine, and now that I've finally officially joined the site I can do this!

Upvote! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: ah. makes sense I guess

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Remember that whole "boys like gross stuff" trend that went around in the media? I think that's why Dr. Wondertainment made them look so gross.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is great and all, but given how horrifying that creature looks and the fact Wondertainment created it for children kind of doesn't make sense. Usually, if WT makes something living and for kids, it looks appealing. but that thing is just horrifying

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That picture is disgusting and I hate it. Had to read the article after that, and I was not disappointed. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Shouldn't we get rid of that "Item SCP-1550" at the beginning of the description?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Good first SCP and all, but the real question is: CAN I HAVE ONE? PLZ!!!!!!!!!!!!

Just joking. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I saw the picture. I upvoted. I then read the article. Excellent use of images and an intriguing concept. Where did you get such a strange picture?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I thought it was adorable.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not bad, but I don't much like the researcher's notes on the different tests. Some of them seem sarcastic and unprofessional.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: God that's hideous… but I want it so much. Even more than those dragon-snails! :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So. What DID happen when Dr. Bright put an egg in a vat of chocolate pudding?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If anyone wants to write that, or any other Tale starring these guys they have my full approval.

As for the "featureless room" test I really have no idea what would happen, but I'm thinking something like Siamese twins, with two specimens merged together sharing organ systems.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Due to the results of the first and second experiments, it might be worth a try to perform an experiment with two of the eggs placed in the same environment and see if they adapt the same, or, like 1550-1 and 1550-2, they adapt in totally different ways.

Another interesting experiment could be placing one in a completely featureless room and seeing what would happen.

I would also be interested to see how they reacted when introduced to each other.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sounds like a story idea.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I just realized the absolute worst and most horrifying possibility:

these things adapt to a Foundation site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not a bad idea at all. I liked pretty much everything about it, but maybe you need to revise the containment procedures, I suggest that maybe the Foundation should adapt some to an apartment enviroment, since these are the safest of all the results.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the idea of "per" as in chemistry meaning "exhaustively" … a perfluorinated compound is one in which every possible hydrogen has been replaced with a fluorine atom, for example.

Neutered is still just spelled wrong, though, and anyone not aware of the chemistry usage wouldn't get the joke anyway.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Cleaned up some obvious errors ("none will not grow" was changed to "none will grow", couple of spelling corrections I happened to notice, and one lab note was changed from "you might as well" to "we might as well").

Things that still need to be changed: I feel that some of the researcher's notes are more informal than would normally be considered appropriate for a laboratory journal, and the last one just bugs me. It wasn't Dr. Wondertainment that decided to try to simulate conditions in the Marianas Trench, buddy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've been considering making this a collaboration page in regards to the test log. Do you think that would be a good idea?

item.title: Scary final test
item.content:encoded:
Possible situations for a horrifying final test (that an Adapto-EggTM could easily wind up in):

  • Electronics or cabling
  • Rolls into a heating duct
  • Toy chest full of army men or other military toys
  • Shelf full of horror media
  • Office supplies
  • Exposure to vermin of any kind
  • Room full of fluffy cutesy toys
  • Fluff under the bed
  • Dirty laundry
  • Put near a TV

I'm sure somebody around here can come up with a hideous result for something on the list.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: At last, a Wondertainment product I would actually want to own that's still creepy enough to be an SCP!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Added the extra information about the eggs.

Also, by "adapt in ways that play off each other" do you mean something like an anthill with differing forms adapted for differing functions or sort of a land siphonophorae. Come to think of it, that sounds like it could be pretty interesting.

http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Siphonophorae

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I kept wondering what "per-netured" meant. Did Doc W invent a propietary form of castration? Or maybe a re-branding so that he can TM the process? Then I figured out it was a typo and felt dumb.

More on topic, if they are born sterile, the containment procedures don't need to include looking for eggs after an escape. Containment should also have something about refrigeration of eggs or some other way of keeping them unhatched. Maybe they come in individual packaging and only hatch after the seal is broken?

The line in the instructions about if the pets get lonely seems to have a lot of potential for squick. Maybe have a heavily redacted experiment or breach incident where several eggs hatch together. The creatures adapt in ways that play off each other, with massivly destructive results. It would fit Wondertainment's style perfectly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Maybe some more warnings about things they shouldn't be exposed to or allowed contact with?

Thought of a creep test to run and a product warning:

Dr. Wondertainment Custom-Pet™ should not be placed into a home with other pets. Dr. Wondertainment is not responsible for possible food chain related injuries or accidents.

I'll just let that simmer for a while, but in the meantime keep away from SCP-247, SCP-549, and SCP-955.

Oh, and if you have the line about intellectual property rights, I'd suggest adding a line about cloning is expressly forbidden.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like this. Weird biologicals always get me. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this one! I would like it even more without the researcher's notes in the test log, and maybe the instructions that come in the package could be fleshed out. Do you have to do anything special to make the eggs hatch? It almost seems like you should, unless something in the packaging is keeping them from hatching. Also, it would be nice to see maybe a little more creep in what these "pets" could become, because otherwise, I want one. Maybe some more warnings about things they shouldn't be exposed to or allowed contact with?

Overall, I enjoyed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Added deep-sea test. Soon as someone thinks up, or I think up an environment for the nasty test one I'll write the test log entry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
o.k. two more experiments coming soon.

Keeping an egg in saltwater pressurized to deep-sea levels via a decompression chamber and an environment which leads to one being DATA EXPUNGED in a really nasty way.

But what environment do you recommend?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This has potential… It has a few typos and formatting I'd like to see fixed. What I'm interested in is what would happen if you put these eggs in an extreme (but not molten) environment where other organisms live. Places like geysers and such, where you get extremophile organisms. Either that or the depths of the oceans. I can see getting some pretty creepy extremophile critters with lots of strange, [REDACTED] characteristics.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fixed the excess censorship about paddles. Also, I got the picture by googling "really alien animal" in the first place and many people like it so for the moment it stays. After all, many other SCPs which are very popular involve random pictures from online. Are you going after Dr. Gears for SCP-217's page images next? I suspect he while is a great writer he didn't put together bug-corpses and clockwork to make those photos. Or his short stories with creepy black and white photos at the bottom? I know I'm no Dr. Gears in terms of writing but if he can do it so can I.

I might add the extra horror form like you said Bogleach however. Perhaps one that was put near a weapon by mistake and developed so it could [DATA EXPUNGED] anyone or anything that threatened it with such a weapon.

EDIT: I think I might have sounded overly hostile defending my first SCP. Sorry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a sculpture, looks like polymer clay

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It does look cool, but it's also clearly somebody's artwork, and if you didn't ask first they could be really upset that someone used their images without any credit. A lot of SCP's use artwork, but if you can find something that wouldn't be so personal, like a close up of a real but unusual animal (try googling "polychaete," they often look pretty alien) it would probably be preferable

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Censoring the danger warnings in their product description makes a sense, but censoring which limbs they turn into paddles feels arbitrary.

I think they need one extra punch of horror. The test log should end with one taking on a form with disturbing implications, when placed in some seemingly innocent location that could easily happen if the eggs were widely distributed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The point of the pre-neutering is to keep them from breeding so Dr. Wondertainment is the only sorce of eggs in existence. I wasn't sure how to make this clear.

As for the picture, I found it online and thought it looked creepy/cool.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just want to know where the *hell* that picture came from; this is one of the few on this site that I honestly can't see as being Of This Earth. Then I look back at some of the crap from the Precambrian era (and a few that survive to this very day) and I want to start investing in flamethrowers.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Actually , I'd say they come pre-neutered to not breed all over your house, not to protect IP rights, or at least it'd be stated more like that.
Hmm, needs more opinions i'd say.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted, excellent job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fixed the "page image" line and will be adding the stuff on the specimens being sterile and the household experiment soon. Great idea Hurtz.

EDIT: It's been edited.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is better now, too bad I can't upvote twice. Pretty good for a first SCP. Keep on it. :)

item.title: Re: Hmh
item.content:encoded:
That's a great point. Dr. Wondertainment would probably fight to reserve the intellectual property rights, so self reproducing pets released would be a no no.

I'm about to delve into the experiment log, but I'm stuck on this:

and none will not grow

This needs a terrarium constructed to look like an apartment for the experiment logs. Will it turn into a creature that burrows into the couch to munch on crumbs found? Will it turn into a closet monster that blends in with a pile of shoes?

Also, agree with TL333s on the experiment 4 note about the page image. Every experiment doesn't need a note, and trying to inject humor can ruin the feel.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Better than it was, good enough for an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Don't ask for upvotes. Let people decide for themselves if they think it's good enough.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think it's been improved. The line about the "page image" feels out of place. I mean, in-verse, these are reports sent to superiors, not articles on a wiki. It would suffice just to change the caption of the image and take that researcher's note out.

item.title: Hmh
item.content:encoded:
Has potential, so upvoted (taking it to -4, and out of deletion territory).

I might want to do some rewriting on this one later, though. Overall concept is interesting.

These things should not reproduce, though. Dr. Wondertainment's pets come pre-neutered.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's all been edited. Please would you kindly upvote now or at least remove your downvotes?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Indeed, I actually think it makes sense to have a certain standard sized cell. When building a site, the Foundation has no idea of the different sizes of SCP it's going to contain. So it makes sense to build a certain number of cells with the same dimensions. Besides, designing a building in which all hundreds of rooms have different dimensions would be a real pain in the ass for the architect. Especially if the height of every cell is different and it's a multi-storey building…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Perhaps every SCP is in a 5m x 5m cell because that's become the Foundation standard? Seems a bit large for some objects, but…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think it has potential, but you are a bit vague in the middle. Also could you choose a different containment size? EVERY SCP is in a 5x5 meter cell. There are plenty of other numbers out there guys.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You've written in a note that SCP-1550-4 is now the page image but haven't changed the image caption.

Also - and I'm not picking fault here, just being curious - you say that the eggs are allowed to hatch; what's stopping them from hatching anyway?

No vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Removed LOLfoundation in notes, got rid of excess redaction, added extra dimensions of container, broke up run-on-sentences and fixed tattos to tattoos. Now what? Also will anyone upvote?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My thoughts exactly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Excellent idea! I would hate to see it go, but I cannot give it an upvote. None of the [DATA EXPUNGED] need to be there, and a lot of what you blacked out is not information that has any apparent reason to be blacked out.

Also… grammar issues abound, along with awkward word placement and usage, and run-on sentences. If you need help identifying or fixing these problems, feel free to message me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That is a pet? Do not want!
